images.caption: Figure 1:Geographic context of the study area. (a) Location of PIK2 within the Indonesian archipelago. (b) High-resolution (1 arc-second,∼30{\sim}30m) bathymetric and topographic relief of the PIK2 domain, showing the low-lying coastal substrate. Data derived from the Shuttle Radar Topography Mission (SRTM)[30], rendered via PyGMT[39].
Figure 2:Annual LULC classification of the PIK2 domain (2017–2024), showing the progressive expansion of Built Area (red) at the expense of Crops (yellow) and natural vegetation (green).
Figure 3:Trajectory of the terrestrial landscape on the Marxian ternary simplex (land pixels only, ocean excluded). Capital surpasses Agrarian between 2020 and 2021.

Figure 4:Information-theoretic analysis of the 7-class LULC distribution. (a) Shannon entropyH​(t)H(t). (b) Rényi entropy spectrumHα​(t)H_{\alpha}(t). (c) KLDDKL​(𝐩​(t+1)∥𝐩​(t))D_{\mathrm{KL}}(\mathbf{p}(t{+}1)\|\mathbf{p}(t)). (d) FR geodesic distancedFR​(𝐩​(t),𝐩​(t+1))d_{\mathrm{FR}}(\mathbf{p}(t),\mathbf{p}(t{+}1)).

Figure 5:Markov chain analysis. (a) Pooled transition probability matrix. (b) Expected absorption time𝔼​[Ti]\mathbb{E}[T_{i}]into Built Area under the absorbing chain formulation. (c) Stationary distribution𝝅\bm{\pi}of the empirical (non-forced) pooled matrix. (d) Built Area self-retention probabilityP​(B→B)P(B\to B)by period, with leakage percentage annotated.

Figure 6:Percolation analysis of the built environment. (a) Order parameterΩ​(t)=Smax/Sbuilt\Omega(t)=S_{\max}/S_{\mathrm{built}}. (b) Number of connected clustersN​(t)N(t). (c) Complementary cumulative distribution function (CCDF) of cluster sizes. (d) Box-counting fractal dimensiondfd_{f}of the largest cluster boundary.
